hero.h1: Plan launches with every team in view
hero.subhead: SignalKit brings tasks, approvals, feedback, and results into one clear workspace so teams can move from draft to live without losing context.

features.heading: One rhythm from idea to rollout
features.subhead: Keep the brief, decisions, and delivery work connected from the first draft to the final report.

items.title: Collect the brief
items.body: Turn goals, owners, dates, and dependencies into a shared launch plan everyone can read.

items.title: Review in context
items.body: Route copy, creative, and product notes through clear checkpoints before anything goes live.

items.title: Measure the launch
items.body: Capture results, blockers, and follow-up work in the same place the launch was planned.

rows.title: Approvals that keep their context
rows.body: Every review carries the brief, the thread, and the deadline with it. Approvers see what changed, what it affects, and what happens next, so sign-off takes minutes instead of another meeting.

rows.title: Reports that write themselves
rows.body: Results, blockers, and follow-ups are captured where the work happened. When the launch ships, the report is already there: what shipped, what slipped, and what the team decided.

developer.heading: Wire SignalKit into your stack
developer.subhead: A small, predictable API. Create launches, push milestones, and pull results from the tools your team already uses.
developer.caption: Works with the CLI, webhooks, and a typed SDK.

specimen.heading: Your workspace, your brand
specimen.subhead: Workspaces pick up your color, type, and controls, so launch pages feel like yours from day one.
specimen.note: Shown in this brand's design language.

stats.label: Team signal
stats.note: Based on active launch workspaces from the last quarter.

trustedBy.lead: Trusted by teams that ship every week

testimonial.quote: SignalKit gave our launch team one place to make decisions, see blockers, and keep momentum without another meeting.
testimonial.name: Maya Chen

caseCard.label: Customer story
caseCard.text: North Pier cut launch review time from nine days to two.

quote2.quote: The Monday status meeting is gone. Nobody misses it.
quote2.name: Priya Anand

pricing.heading: Pricing that scales with the launch calendar
pricing.subhead: Start free. Upgrade when the whole team plans in SignalKit.
pricing.note: Prices in USD. Every tier includes unlimited viewers.

items.a: Most teams run their first launch the same week. Import a plan or start from the launch template, invite the team, and go.
Yes. Approvers get a link, review in the browser, and never need an account to sign off.
They become searchable reports. Every decision, file, and result stays attached to the launch that produced it.

items.q: Is there a limit on team size?
items.a: No. Every tier includes unlimited viewers; you only pay for editors.

cta.heading: Bring the next launch into focus
cta.subhead: Create a shared workspace for plans, reviews, approvals, and results.

form.heading: Start a conversation
form.subhead: Tell us about your launch and the team will follow up within a day.

message.label: How can we help?
message.placeholder: Tell us what you are launching.

form.note: A demo form. Nothing is sent.

footer.description: A shared workspace for planning, approving, and measuring product launches.
